docs.title: hashreport
docs.text:
Warning

This documentation site is an early work in progress and may contain errors and incomplete information.

docs.title: Overview
docs.text: hashreport is a command-line tool that generates comprehensive hash reports for files within a directory. The reports can be output in CSV or JSON formats and include detailed information such as the file name, path, size, hash algorithm, hash value, and last modified date. Designed for speed and efficiency, hashreport can handle large volumes of files and offers filtering options to include or exclude files based on size, type, or name patterns.

docs.title: Features
docs.text:
• Bulk hash generation for large directories
  • Support for multiple hash algorithms using hashlib
  • Multi-threaded processing for faster performance
  • Test run mode to process a subset of files
  • Output reports in CSV and JSON formats
  • Filter files by size, type, and name patterns
  • Option to provide a file list for inclusion or exclusion
  • Recursive directory traversal to process nested folders
  • Email report upon completion using SMTP

docs.title: Installation
docs.text: See the Installation page for detailed instructions on how to install hashreport.

docs.title: License

docs.title: Issues and Feedback
docs.text: Please report any issues or feedback on the GitHub Issues page.

docs.title: Project Scripts
docs.text: The scripts directory contains scripts that can be used for various common project actions. These scripts are intended to be run from the project root directory.

docs.title: Run Script
docs.text:
scripts/run.sh

The run.sh script is the main entry point for running project scripts, however, the scripts in the actions directory can also be run directly if desired.

docs.title: Options
docs.text:
The run.sh script provides a quick way to run various project actions. The following options are available:

  • test: run tests with coverage
  • security: check for security issues
  • lint: lint the code
  • format: format code
  • install: install dependencies

docs.title: Usage
docs.text:
To run a specific action, use the following command:

bash scripts/run.sh [OPTION] [FLAGS]

docs.title: Install Dependencies
docs.text:
scripts/install.sh

Installs Poetry (if needed) and then installs project dependencies. The script can be run with the following command:

bash scripts/run.sh install
To automatically install all dependencies, use the -a or --all flag:

bash scripts/run.sh install -a

docs.title: Testing with Coverage
docs.text:
scripts/actions/test.sh

Lint the code and run tests with coverage. This script can be run with the following command:

bash scripts/run.sh test

docs.title: Security Check
docs.text:
scripts/actions/security.sh

Check for security vulnerabilities with Bandit. This script can be run with the following command:

bash scripts/run.sh security

docs.title: Linting
docs.text:
scripts/actions/lint.sh

Lint the code using flake8. This script can be run with the following command:

bash scripts/run.sh lint

docs.title: Code Formatting
docs.text:
scripts/actions/format.sh

Format code with Black and sort imports with isort. This script can be run with the following command:

bash scripts/run.sh format

docs.title: 1. Download the Repository
docs.text:
Clone the repository to your local machine using Git and navigate to the project directory:

git clone https://github.com/madebyjake/hashreport.git && cd hashreport

Alternatively, you can download the repository as a ZIP file and extract it to a folder on your machine.

docs.title: 2. Install Dependencies
docs.text:
First we'll install Poetry, a Python packaging and dependency management tool. There are a few ways to do this, but the recommended method is to use the installer script:

curl -sSL https://install.python-poetry.org | python3 -

Next, install the project dependencies using Poetry:

poetry install

docs.title: 3. Run the Application
docs.text:
You can now run the application using Poetry:

poetry run hashreport --help
